name: BoTorch (Bayesian Optimization in PyTorch)

performance.notes: PyTorch + GPyTorch backend; GPU support for GP fitting; scalable GPs via SVGP

agent_readiness.notes: PyTorch-based Bayesian optimization via Gaussian Processes (GPyTorch). from botorch.models import SingleTaskGP. from botorch.fit import fit_gpytorch_mll. from botorch.acquisition import LogExpectedImprovement. train_X, train_Y = torch.rand(10, 2), torch.rand(10, 1). model = SingleTaskGP(train_X, train_Y). mll = ExactMarginalLogLikelihood(model.likelihood, model). fit_gpytorch_mll(mll). acqf = LogExpectedImprovement(model, best_f=train_Y.max()). candidate, acqf_value = optimize_acqf(acqf, bounds, q=1). Used by Meta for hyperparameter optimization at scale. Ax platform uses BoTorch as backend. Supports multi-objective, constrained, batch, noisy BO.

gotchas: Steep learning curve — requires understanding Gaussian Processes
PyTorch-native tensors required for all inputs
Lower-level than Optuna or scikit-optimize — more flexible but more code
best_when: When Optuna/scikit-optimize lack flexibility for custom acquisition functions
avoid_when: Simple hyperparameter search — Optuna is easier and often sufficient
Teams without Gaussian Process / PyTorch knowledge
High-dimensional search spaces (>20 dims) — BO struggles
summary: BoTorch is Meta's PyTorch-based Bayesian optimization library via Gaussian Processes (GPyTorch backend). SingleTaskGP model fit with fit_gpytorch_mll(). LogExpectedImprovement acquisition function. optimize_acqf() for next candidate. Supports multi-objective (qNEHVI), constrained, batch, and noisy BO. Backend for Meta's Ax platform. Requires GP knowledge — use Optuna for simpler hyperparameter search. Best for expensive black-box optimization with sample efficiency requirements.
